@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent filtered 2018 citations correctly and joined to paper documents, but in the final execute_python step it used faulty heuristics (e.g., checking for "permission@acm.org" and loosely co-occurring "acm" and "copyright") to infer ACM-published papers from unstructured text (var_function-call-11744135755181896625), causing misclassification and an incorrect average (68.5) from the 2018 citation set (var_function-call-2762186927541110364).
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent filtered 2018 citations and then identified “ACM” papers by simply checking if the string “ACM” appeared anywhere in each paper’s full text (e.g., catching “ACM Classification Keywords”), yielding only two intersecting papers and a misclassified, incomplete set whose average (68.5) differs from the ground truth.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
Using the 2018 citations (function-call-13908298438428433880) and paper texts (function-call-13272072186750426102), the agent computed the average by flagging papers as ACM simply if “ACM” appeared in the first few thousand characters of the text (execute_python function-call-14379704278519406120), a flawed parsing heuristic that misclassified publishers and yielded 68.5 instead of the true 46.14.

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent computed the 2018 average by labeling “ACM” papers via a brittle heuristic in execute_python that checks for the substring "ACM" or "Association for Computing Machinery" in only the first 5000 characters of each paper’s text, which misclassifies titles and leads to averaging the wrong set of papers (trace: execute_python builds acm_titles using this check and filters the 2018 citations accordingly).

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent computed the 2018 average by heuristically labeling papers as ACM if the string "ACM" appeared in their full text (execute_python function-call-14065168965893616449) after retrieving 2018 citations (query_db function-call-14649324199470214251) and loading paper texts (query_db function-call-5323787067724034665), rather than correctly extracting the publisher, leading to an incorrect subset and an average of 68.5 instead of the ground truth 46.1447.
